SENTIMENT: Mixed
STRENGTHS: The regular coffees were very good, better than many. The Amazonia and the Africana medium roasts were liked. The dark roast Eclipse, Mexican, and French roast were very good. The chocolate raspberry flavor went nicely into a chocolate cake batter in place of water and it was delicious. There is a wonderful selection of coffees.
COMPLAINTS: The flavored coffees taste artificial and weird. The Costa Rican light roast had an artificial taste. The house blend was good but nothing to write home about. The decaf was not liked. Some flavors were not included in the package. There were duplicates of ordinary coffees received.

Reviews:
it only takes a cursory look at my reviewing history to realize that i haven't exactly made a career out of rating gum, but this new trident vitality is so darn good that i thought i'd better get in a few words on it. i've tried all three of the available flavors and can't seem to choose a favorite. the peppermint with ginseng flavor reviewed here is definitely pretty awesome. the gum has the physical profile of an old-school chicklet. it only takes a bite or two for the strong peppermint flavor to kick in. and man, does this gum last. i can usually go a good 3-4 hours and still get some flavor out of this stuff. that makes it a good value in my book for sure. typically retails for around 1.39 pack at my local grocer, although i've caught it on sale for under a dollar once or twice.
